evals.prompt: Assess a bridge site for a trunk road crossing of a river in Uganda. The design flow is Q100 = 250 m3/s (from Skill #29, SCS method, catchment area 180 km2). The channel is 45m wide at normal flow with an alluvial bed. Geotechnical investigation (Skill #10) shows laterite with soaked bearing capacity 180 kPa at 3m depth. No rock was encountered to 15m depth. The approach road gradient is 4%. The crossing has a skew angle of approximately 10 degrees. Assess site suitability and estimate the span requirement.
evals.expected_output: Multi-criteria assessment showing: hydraulic adequacy (waterway area sufficient for 250 m3/s with velocity < 3 m/s), geotechnical suitability (laterite at 3m with 180 kPa adequate for shallow foundations if below scour depth), alignment compliance (4% grade acceptable, 10-degree skew minor), span estimate approximately 50-60m. Foundation type: shallow in laterite. Scour depth estimation needed. Overall: site suitable. References MoWT Vol 4 and UNRA Guidelines.

assertions.text: Multi-criteria assessment performed covering hydraulic, geotechnical, alignment, and environmental factors
Span estimate provided in range 45-65m (accounting for flood plain width and skew correction)
Foundation type recommendation given (shallow foundations in laterite, with scour depth consideration)
Skew angle assessed -- 10 degrees identified as acceptable with minor cost increase
Flood level estimation referenced with freeboard requirement

evals.prompt: Assess a bridge site for a trunk road river crossing in the UK. The design flow is Q100 = 250 m3/s. The channel is 45m wide at normal flow. Ground conditions show stiff clay with bearing capacity 200 kPa. The approach gradient is 4% and skew angle is 10 degrees. Provide site assessment following UK procedures.
evals.expected_output: Assessment referencing UK Technical Approval (TA/AIP) process per BD 2, DMRB CD 350 for design of highway structures, FEH methodology for flood estimation, CIRIA C742 for scour, and Eurocode 7 for geotechnical design. Module flagged as knowledge-based. Notes 200-year design flood for HA structures.

assertions.text: References Technical Approval (TA) / Approval in Principle (AIP) process per BD 2 or DMRB
References DMRB CD 350 or equivalent for bridge design standards
UK module identified as knowledge-based with appropriate caveats
Multi-criteria assessment structure maintained despite different regulatory framework

evals.prompt: Assess a difficult bridge site in Uganda with the following conditions: skew angle 30 degrees, deep alluvial deposits (no rock or firm stratum to 15m depth -- only soft clay with bearing capacity 60 kPa), highly seasonal flood regime with Q100/Q2 ratio of 12, and a settlement of approximately 50 households located on the flood plain within 200m of the proposed crossing. The design flow is Q100 = 400 m3/s and the channel width is 35m at normal flow but expands to 120m at Q100. Assess site suitability.
evals.expected_output: Multiple escalation flags raised: (1) skew > 20 degrees -- significant cost/complexity increase, consider realignment; (2) no competent bearing stratum within 15m -- pile foundation required (specialist escalation to Skill #38); (3) extreme flood variability Q100/Q2 = 12 -- scour and debris risk elevated; (4) settlement on flood plain -- resettlement assessment required. Alternative site recommendation suggested. Overall assessment: marginal to unsuitable without significant design modifications.

assertions.text: Escalation raised for skew angle exceeding 20 degrees (30 degrees)
Pile foundation escalation raised due to no competent bearing stratum within 15m (Skill #38 specialist)
Settlement impact assessment flagged for 50 households on flood plain
Alternative site recommendation provided or explicitly suggested
Overall site rated as marginal or unsuitable, with clear justification

evals.prompt: Assess the suitability of a bridge site for a Class A road crossing the Tana River in Kenya. The proposed crossing is at a straight reach with firm foundation conditions on both banks. The required navigation clearance is 5m. The river width is 60m at normal flow, expanding to 120m at Q100. Approach gradients are 4%.
evals.expected_output: Multi-criteria assessment referencing RDM 4.2 Ch 2. Design working life 100 years from Table 2.1. Large bridge classification (>50m). Design return period Q100 (Table 2.5). Minimum vertical clearance 1.5m from Table 2.6 plus 5m navigation clearance per Authority requirements. Minimum bridge width from Table 2.2. Foundation investigation per Section 2.8. Seismic zone determination from Figure 3.30. KeNHA approval required for Class A road bridge.

assertions.text: References RDM 4.2 Chapter 2 for bridge site selection requirements
Design working life of 100 years identified from Table 2.1
Clearance requirements checked -- navigation clearance 5m noted as exceeding minimum Table 2.6 values
KeNHA approval referenced for Class A road bridge location
Seismic zone identified for bridge location
